item.dc:creator: Ben Kamisar
Bridget Bowman
item.description: President Donald Trump’s campaign to suppress dissent in his own party faces its most prominent test yet as he takes aim at Rep. Thomas Massie in Kentucky, while GOP candidates across the country look to leverage the president’s endorsement in key primaries.

media:content.media:text: DHS Oversight House 3/04/26
media:content.media:description: Rep. Thomas Massie, R-Ky., during the House Judiciary Committee hearing titled "Oversight of the Department of Homeland Security," in Rayburn building on March 3, 2026.

media:content.media:text: DOnald Trump, left, and Burt Jones speak near a crowd of people inside of a restaurant
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump and Georgia Lt. Gov. Burt Jones together in Rome, Ga., on Feb. 19.

item.title: Trump loyalty tests, major battlegrounds and more: What to watch in Tuesday’s elections

item.dc:creator: Ryan J. Reilly
Gary Grumbach
Dareh Gregorian
item.description: The addendum comes a day after the Justice Department announced that Trump and his co-plaintiffs would drop their $10 billion suit against the IRS.

media:content.media:text: Trump Says Ballroom To Include Military Hospital, Research Site
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump reached an agreement with his own administration Monday after he dropped a $10 billion lawsuit against the IRS.

item.title: Justice Department agrees not to pursue any tax claims against Trump as part of IRS deal

item.description: Investigators say they are looking into a 75-page manifesto left by the suspects in the San Diego mosque shooting. The documents, which authorities say are filled with extremist material against minorities and praise the shooter in the Christchurch massacre in New Zealand, were found in the suspects' vehicle. NBC News correspondent Tom Winter outlines some of the details of the documents, which NBC News has reviewed.

media:content.media:title: Investigators looking into manifesto left by San Diego shooting suspects
media:content.media:description: Investigators say they are looking into a 75-page manifesto left by the suspects in the San Diego mosque shooting. The documents, which authorities say are filled with extremist material against minorities and praise the shooter in the Christchurch massacre in New Zealand, were found in the suspects' vehicle. NBC News correspondent Tom Winter outlines some of the details of the documents, which NBC News has reviewed.

item.title: Investigators looking into manifesto left by San Diego shooting suspects

item.description: The CDC confirms an American doctor has tested positive for the deadly Ebola virus while working in the Democratic Republic of Congo. This comes as entry into the United States is being restricted for some travelers in response to the outbreak. NBC’s Molly Hunter reports for TODAY.

media:content.media:title: American Doctor Infected With Ebola While Working in Congo
media:content.media:description: The CDC confirms an American doctor has tested positive for the deadly Ebola virus while working in the Democratic Republic of Congo. This comes as entry into the United States is being restricted for some travelers in response to the outbreak. NBC’s Molly Hunter reports for TODAY.

item.title: American Doctor Infected With Ebola While Working in Congo

item.dc:creator: Andrew Greif
item.description: Arsenal’s two-decade absence atop the Premier League has ended, giving one of the world’s biggest and richest clubs the title it had long sought but narrowly missed for years.

media:content.media:text: Arsenal v Burnley - Premier League
media:content.media:description: Declan Rice of Arsenal celebrates at the full time whistle during the Premier League match between Arsenal and Burnley at Emirates Stadium on May 18, 2026 in London, England.

media:content.media:text: Arsenal v Burnley - Premier League - Emirates Stadium
media:content.media:description: Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta hugs Arsenal mascot Gunnersaurus after the Premier League match at the Emirates Stadium, London, on Monday.

item.title: Arsenal win Premier League title for first time in 22 years

item.description: President Trump took questions from the press about the conflict with Iran after giving a tour of the White House ballroom construction site. NBC News' Gabe Gutierrez reports on the president's decision not to strike Iran and ballroom developments.

media:content.media:title: Trump talks Iran while giving tour of White House ballroom construction
media:content.media:description: President Trump took questions from the press about the conflict with Iran after giving a tour of the White House ballroom construction site. NBC News' Gabe Gutierrez reports on the president's decision not to strike Iran and ballroom developments.

item.title: Trump talks Iran while giving tour of White House ballroom construction

item.dc:creator: Michael Kosnar
Kelly O'Donnell
Tim Stelloh
Nicole Acevedo
item.description: A U.S. indictment of former Cuban president Raúl Castro is expected to be announced Wednesday in Miami, two federal sources familiar with the investigation told NBC News

media:content.media:text: Raul Castro.
media:content.media:description: Raul Castro in Havana on May 1, 2026.

item.title: Raúl Castro is expected to be indicted by U.S. on Wednesday, sources say

item.dc:creator: Frank Thorp V
Brennan Leach
Sahil Kapur
item.description: The Senate voted to move forward with a resolution to force President Donald Trump to end the war in Iran, a breakthrough for the Democratic-led effort.

media:content.media:text: Sen. Bill Cassidy.
media:content.media:description: Sen. Bill Cassidy, R-La., voted to stop the Iran war for the first time after losing his primary.

item.title: Senate advances resolution to end Iran war as GOP Sen. Bill Cassidy flips to support it

item.dc:creator: Ryan J. Reilly
item.description: Months before the $1.7 billion fund was announced, Justice Department official Ed Martin said Capitol rioters would get millions, even if it took until 2028.

media:content.media:credit: Craig Hudson for The Washington Post via Getty Images file
media:content.media:text: Ed Martin speaks during a press conference
media:content.media:description: Supporters of President Donald Trump rally at the U.S. Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021.

media:content.media:text: Ed Martin.
media:content.media:description: Supporters of President Donald Trump rally at the U.S. Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021.

media:content.media:text: Donald Trump.
media:content.media:description: Supporters of President Donald Trump rally at the U.S. Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021.

item.title: DOJ official told GOP ally that big payouts were coming for Jan. 6 defendants

item.dc:creator: Emily Lorsch
item.description: Meta is moving 7,000 employees into roles focused on AI and consolidating them into four new organizations, according to a source familiar with the matter.

media:content.media:text: Mark Zuckerberg.
media:content.media:description: Mark Zuckerberg, CEO of Meta, in Washington on March 26.

item.title: As Meta lays off 10%, 7,000 employees will be moved into AI roles, source says

item.dc:creator: Madison Lambert
Tim Stelloh
item.description: A woman fell to her death in New York City after she accidentally stepped into an open manhole, authorities said.

media:content.media:text: Manhole cover at a crime scene.
media:content.media:description: A freak accident in midtown Manhattan killed a 56-year-old woman just before midnight Tuesday.

item.title: Woman dies in NYC after stepping into open manhole

item.dc:creator: The Politics Desk
item.description: Steve Kornacki dives into the key places to watch in Rep. Thomas Massie’s district tonight as he tries to fend off a GOP primary challenger backed by President Donald Trump.

media:content.media:text: Image: DHS Secretary Kristi Noem Testifies During House Judiciary Committee Hearing
media:content.media:description: Rep. Thomas Massie speaks as U.S. Secretary of Homeland Security Kristi Noem testifies during a House Judiciary Committee hearing in the Rayburn House Office Building on March 4, 2026 in Washington, D.C.

item.title: What to watch for tonight in Thomas Massie’s unique district: From the Politics Desk

item.dc:creator: Ryan J. Reilly
Gary Grumbach
Dareh Gregorian
item.description: The addendum comes a day after the Justice Department announced that Trump and his co-plaintiffs would drop their $10 billion suit against the IRS.

media:content.media:text: Trump Says Ballroom To Include Military Hospital, Research Site
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump reached an agreement with his own administration Monday after he dropped a $10 billion lawsuit against the IRS.

item.title: Justice Department agrees not to pursue any tax claims against Trump as part of IRS deal

item.dc:creator: Andrew Greif
item.description: Arsenal’s two-decade absence atop the Premier League has ended, giving one of the world’s biggest and richest clubs the title it had long sought but narrowly missed for years.

media:content.media:text: Arsenal v Burnley - Premier League
media:content.media:description: Declan Rice of Arsenal celebrates at the full time whistle during the Premier League match between Arsenal and Burnley at Emirates Stadium on May 18, 2026 in London, England.

media:content.media:text: Arsenal v Burnley - Premier League - Emirates Stadium
media:content.media:description: Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta hugs Arsenal mascot Gunnersaurus after the Premier League match at the Emirates Stadium, London, on Monday.

item.title: Arsenal win Premier League title for first time in 22 years

item.dc:creator: Allan Smith
Sahil Kapur
item.description: Sen. Josh Hawley, R-Mo., said he would like to see presidents — along with other federal officials — banned from trading individual stocks.

media:content.media:text: Josh Hawley 10/16/25
media:content.media:description: Sen. Josh Hawley, R-Mo., talks with reporters in the U.S. Capitol during votes related to the government shutdown on Oct. 16, 2025.

item.title: Sen. Josh Hawley says he favors a stock trading ban that applies to presidents

item.dc:creator: Minyvonne Burke
item.description: A New Jersey man was sentenced Tuesday to life in prison without the possibility of parole after he was convicted of murdering his brother and his brother’s family in what the judge said were “brutal” and “calculated” killings.

media:content.media:text: Paul Caneiro during his trial before State Superior Court Judge Marc C. Lemieux on Friday, Feb., 6, 2026, at the Monmouth County Courthouse in Freehold, NJ.
media:content.media:description: Paul Caneiro during his trial on Feb. 6 at the Monmouth County Courthouse in Freehold, N.J.

item.title: N.J. man gets life in prison for ‘calculated’ murder of brother and his family

item.dc:creator: Ashley Morris
item.description: I rounded up the best deals this week from Ulta, J. Crew, Home Depot and more on sunscreen, summer clothes, outdoor furniture and more.

media:content.media:text: You can save on bestsellers from Ulta, J. Crew, Ilia and more.
media:content.media:description: You can save on bestsellers from Ulta, J. Crew, Ilia and more.

media:content.media:text: You can save on bestsellers from Ulta, J. Crew, Ilia and more.
media:content.media:description: You can save on bestsellers from Ulta, J. Crew, Ilia and more.

item.title: 68+ best sales we plan to shop this week: Ilia, J. Crew and more

item.dc:creator: Zoe Malin
item.description: Looking for a dentist-approved mouthwash? Experts recommend top options for fresh breath and healthy gums.

media:content.media:text: Mouthwashes are made with different ingredients depending on what their purpose is, like fighting cavities, reducing bad breath and preventing gingivitis.
media:content.media:description: Mouthwashes are made with different ingredients depending on what their purpose is, like fighting cavities, reducing bad breath and preventing gingivitis.

media:content.media:text: Mouthwashes are made with different ingredients depending on what their purpose is, like fighting cavities, reducing bad breath and preventing gingivitis.
media:content.media:description: Mouthwashes are made with different ingredients depending on what their purpose is, like fighting cavities, reducing bad breath and preventing gingivitis.

media:content.media:text: Brown likes that this Listerine mouthwash doesn’t irritate her gums.
media:content.media:description: Mouthwashes are made with different ingredients depending on what their purpose is, like fighting cavities, reducing bad breath and preventing gingivitis.

media:content.media:text: Hand holding a blue bottle of Therabreath mouthwash.
media:content.media:description: Mouthwashes are made with different ingredients depending on what their purpose is, like fighting cavities, reducing bad breath and preventing gingivitis.

media:content.media:text: White bottle of Carifree mouthwash on a black desk.
media:content.media:description: Mouthwashes are made with different ingredients depending on what their purpose is, like fighting cavities, reducing bad breath and preventing gingivitis.

item.title: If you brush and floss, do you really need mouthwash? Here’s what dentists say

item.dc:creator: Zoe Malin
item.description: Bravo’s Lindsay Hubbard shares her all-time favorite products. Plus, what to expect from the ‘Summer House’ season 10 finale and ‘In The City.”

media:content.media:text: Lindsay Hubbard from Bravo’s ‘Summer House’ and ‘In the City’ series says some of her favorite products are from Breville, YSL and Aveeno.
media:content.media:description: Lindsay Hubbard from Bravo’s ‘Summer House’ and ‘In the City’ series says some of her favorite products are from Breville, YSL and Aveeno.

item.title: Talking Shop with ‘Summer House’ star Lindsay Hubbard

item.dc:creator: Bethany Heitman
Bianca Alvarez
item.description: Experts explain how to choose the best SPF lip balm and which ingredients to keep lips moisturized and protected. Shop Sun Bum, Aquaphor and more.

media:content.media:text: We spoke with doctors to learn the importance of using SPF lip balm and which ingredients best hydrate and protect your lips.
media:content.media:description: We spoke with doctors to learn the importance of using SPF lip balm and which ingredients best hydrate and protect your lips.

media:content.media:text: We spoke with doctors to learn the importance of using SPF lip balm and which ingredients best hydrate and protect your lips.

media:content.media:text: Image of a woman holding lip balm next to her face after applying it.
media:content.media:description: We spoke with doctors to learn the importance of using SPF lip balm and which ingredients best hydrate and protect your lips.

item.title: The best SPF lip balms to hydrate and protect your lips

Sahil Kapur
item.description: Sen. Josh Hawley, R-Mo., said he would like to see presidents — along with other federal officials — banned from trading individual stocks.

media:content.media:text: Josh Hawley 10/16/25

item.title: Sen. Josh Hawley says he favors a stock trading ban that applies to presidents

item.dc:creator: Gordon Lubold
Mosheh Gains
item.description: The inspector general will look at the intelligence and targeting Southern Command used for the strikes, as well as other aspects of the operations against drug cartels, according to a May 11 letter from the Pentagon’s Office of Inspector General.

media:content.media:text: The Pentagon in Arlington, Virginia.
media:content.media:description: The Pentagon launched Operation Southern Spear, its name for the campaign against alleged drug-smuggling boats, in 2025.

media:content.media:text: Smoke rises from a boat after a U.S. strike in the Eastern Pacific on April 13, 2026.
media:content.media:description: The Pentagon launched Operation Southern Spear, its name for the campaign against alleged drug-smuggling boats, in 2025.

item.title: Pentagon’s internal watchdog to probe U.S. strikes on alleged drug boats

item.dc:creator: Jane C. Timm
Amanda Terkel
item.description: Democratic Gov. Abigail Spanberger said she plans to issue an executive order Wednesday to help election workers respond if federal agents show up at polling sites in Virginia.

media:content.media:text: State of the Union Democrats
media:content.media:description: Virginia Gov. Abigail Spanberger.

item.title: Virginia Gov. Abigail Spanberger to sign order responding to federal agents at polls

item.dc:creator: Bracey Harris
item.description: The NAACP is urging Black student-athletes to withhold their college commitments in states where new congressional maps could be drawn.

media:content.media:text: NAACP President Derrick Johnson at the NAACP National Convention in Boston, M.A. on July 28, 2023.
media:content.media:description: NAACP President Derrick Johnson at the civil rights organization's national convention in Boston, in 2023.

item.title: NAACP calls for Black student-athletes to boycott Southern schools amid redistricting backlash

item.dc:creator: Jane C. Timm
item.description: A group of news organizations, including NBC News, has filed a motion to unseal filings and subpoenas in the Department of Justice’s ongoing investigation into the 2020 election in Fulton County, Georgia

media:content.media:text: Two gloved hands hold absentee ballots
media:content.media:description: A worker at the Fulton County Board of Registration and Elections processes absentee ballots at the State Farm Arena in Atlanta in November 2020.

item.title: News organizations sue to unseal filings in DOJ’s Fulton County election investigation
